title: Estimating Inventory: Gross Profit Method
event: A flood destroys Ortiz Co.'s warehouse. Records show beginning inventory of $50,000, purchases of $110,000, and net sales of $150,000. The company's gross profit ratio is 30%. Compute the estimated ending inventory destroyed.

computationPaths.narration: Work the COGS equation backwards: estimate COGS from the sales and the GP ratio, then subtract from goods available.

memorize.body: Estimated COGS = Net sales × (1 − GP ratio). Estimated EI = Goods available − Estimated COGS.
The GP ratio is a percentage of SALES — so the cost side of sales is (1 − ratio), not the ratio itself.

questions.prompt: GAFS $160,000, net sales $150,000, GP ratio 30%. Estimated ending inventory = ?

distractors.feedback: That's the estimated GROSS PROFIT (150,000 × 30%). COGS is the other 70%: 105,000, leaving 55,000 of inventory.
You subtracted full sales from goods available — sales are at RETAIL. Convert to cost first: 150,000 × 70% = 105,000.
